title: Project: Start the Field Notes Repository
summary: Turn a small set of neighborhood field notes into a trustworthy first Git repository with a clean initial commit.

sketchBlocks.title: Understanding Git Repositories
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
A Git repository is a storage space where your project files and their history are kept. It allows you to track changes, collaborate with others, and revert to previous states if needed. In this project, you'll learn how to set up a Git repository for a neighborhood garden's field notes, ensuring that all changes are tracked and the initial state is preserved.

### Project Brief



**Scenario:** A neighborhood garden coordinator keeps plot observations in a folder, but every revision currently replaces the previous one. The next volunteer needs a trustworthy starting point before more notes are added.

**Your role:** Junior developer preparing the garden field-notes repository

**Workspace:** One cumulative terminal workspace rooted at garden-notes/ with README.md, observations/, and a deterministic local Git identity

**Deliverable:** A five-step repository setup ending with an initialized main branch, tracked project files, one meaningful initial commit, and a clean working tree.

quizDraft.title: Inspect the Garden Notes Workspace
quizDraft.prompt:
Use `pwd` and `ls` to inspect the `garden-notes/` directory. Read the provided `README.md` and observation files before making any changes. Type each command and press Enter.

This practice opens in a fresh workspace. First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter, then type each requested Git command and press Enter.
quizDraft.hint: Use `pwd` to check your current directory and `ls` to list files.

help.concept: Understanding the current directory and its contents is crucial before initializing a repository.
help.hint_1: Use `pwd` to confirm you're in the correct directory.
help.hint_2: Use `ls` to see the files available in the directory.

# First type: bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
# Press Enter, then complete the requested Git task.

starterFiles.content:
# First type: bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
# Press Enter, then complete the requested Git task.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
git init -q -b main
git config user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
printf '.zoeskoul/
main.sh
' >> .git/info/exclude
printf 'Git practice file 1\n' > README.md

quizDraft.title: Configure the Local Author Identity
quizDraft.prompt:
Set the local Git identity for the `garden-notes/` repository. Use `git config --local` to set `user.name` to "ZoeSkoul Learner" and `user.email` to "learner@zoeskoul.local". Type each command and press Enter.

This practice opens in a fresh workspace. First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter, then type each requested Git command and press Enter.
quizDraft.hint: Use the lesson explanation and the wording of this question to narrow the answer.

This practice opens in a fresh workspace. First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter, then type each requested Git command and press Enter.

quizDraft.title: Stage the Initial Project Files
quizDraft.prompt:
Stage the `README.md` and all files in the `observations/` directory. Use `git add` to prepare these files for commit. Type each command and press Enter.

This practice opens in a fresh workspace. First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter, then type each requested Git command and press Enter.
quizDraft.hint: Read the Git task "Stage the Initial Project Files" and identify the repository state you need to create.

help.concept: This Git exercise checks the repository state produced by "Stage the Initial Project Files".
help.hint_1: Use git status before and after each small change so you can see what moved between the working tree, staging area, and history.
help.hint_2: Match the requested file names, branch names, and commit purpose before clicking Check Answer.

quizDraft.starterCode:
# First type: bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
# Press Enter, then complete the requested Git task.
quizDraft.solutionCode:

git add
git add README.md
git add observations/

quizDraft.title: Create and Verify the First Commit
quizDraft.prompt:
Commit the staged files with the message "Start garden field notes". Use `git commit` and verify the commit with `git log --oneline`. Type each command and press Enter.

This practice opens in a fresh workspace. First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter, then type each requested Git command and press Enter.
quizDraft.hint: Use the lesson explanation and the wording of this question to narrow the answer.

help.concept: This support text was repaired because the original wording revealed the answer too directly.

quizDraft.starterCode:
# First type: bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
# Press Enter, then complete the requested Git task.
quizDraft.solutionCode:
bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
git commit
git log --oneline
git commit -m
git commit -m "Start garden field notes"
